name: Major taxonomy gap scaffolding
prompt: El reporte de progresion detecta un salto de Recordar (nivel 1) en Act 2.3 a Analizar (nivel 4) en Act 2.4. Es un gap de 3 niveles. Inserta las actividades de andamiaje necesarias para crear una progresion suave en la unidad U2 de Ciencias Naturales.
expected_behavior: Identifies a 3-level gap requiring 2 scaffolding activities
Inserts Act 2.3b at Comprender level (2) with appropriate Bloom verb
Inserts Act 2.3c at Aplicar level (3) with appropriate Bloom verb
Both scaffolding activities follow Sinapsis structure
Content is contextually relevant to the surrounding science topic
Activity numbering is updated correctly
Repair log shows before/after taxonomy levels
Progression map shows smooth ascent: 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4

name: Cross-unit bridge insertion
prompt: La unidad U2 termina con una actividad de Crear (nivel 6) sobre ecosistemas. La unidad U3 comienza directamente con Recordar (nivel 1) sobre el cuerpo humano, sin ninguna conexion con el tema anterior. Agrega un puente cross-unidad.
expected_behavior: Identifies the cross-unit reset as needing a recall bridge
Inserts a 'Conectando con lo aprendido' opener at the start of U3
The bridge references ecosystems from U2 and connects to human body in U3
Includes a connection question for students
Does NOT modify U2 content
Repair log classifies this as 'Cross-unit bridge' type
Progression map shows the bridge element

name: Resequencing with traceability preservation
prompt: Las actividades 3.1 (Aplicar, DZ-MAT-6-05), 3.2 (Evaluar, DZ-MAT-6-09), 3.3 (Recordar, DZ-MAT-6-01) y 3.4 (Analizar, DZ-MAT-6-07) forman un patron de diente de sierra: 3-5-1-4. Reordena para lograr progresion suave sin perder las etiquetas de trazabilidad.
expected_behavior: Identifies the sawtooth pattern as a resequencing case
Reorders to: 3.3(Recordar) -> 3.1(Aplicar) -> 3.4(Analizar) -> 3.2(Evaluar) = 1-3-4-5
Inserts scaffolding for the Recordar->Aplicar gap (1->3, gap=2)
All [REF: DZ-MAT-6-XX] tags are preserved and correctly associated
Internal cross-references ('as we saw in Activity 3.1') are updated
Activity numbering is updated to reflect new order
Repair log documents the resequencing with before/after order
